text: 6 Minute English from bbclearningenglish .com
zh: 来自bbclearningenglish.com的六分钟英语

text: Hello. This is 6 Minute English from BBC Learning English.
zh: 大家好，欢迎收听BBC英语教学的《六分钟英语》。

text: I'm Georgie. And I'm Phil. Did you have any breakfast today, Georgie? Or did you skip it?
zh: 我是乔治。我是菲尔。乔治，你今天吃早饭了吗？还是没吃？

text: I did have some breakfast. On the way to the office, I got myself a pastry and a coffee.
zh: 我吃了一点早饭。在来办公室的路上，我买了一个糕点和一杯咖啡。

text: What about you? I had a big bowl of porridge, but it was at six o 'clock this morning, so I'm hungry again. again now.
zh: 你呢？我吃了一大碗粥，不过那是今天早上六点的事了，所以我现在又饿了。

text: And you might soon be hungry as well because we're going to talk about breakfast.
zh: 你可能很快也会觉得饿，因为我们今天要聊的话题就是早餐。

text: How important is it? Let's start by hearing from Professor Alexandra Johnston,
zh: 早餐到底有多重要呢？我们先来听听亚历山德拉·约翰斯顿教授怎么说，

text: a nutrition scientist who spoke to Ruth Alexander, host of BBC World Service programme, The Food Chain.
zh: 她是一位营养学家，曾接受BBC世界服务节目《The Food Chain》主持人露丝·亚历山大的采访。

text: I am supporting the meme, breakfast like a king and dine like a pauper.
zh: 我支持这样一句流行说法：早餐要吃得像国王，晚餐要吃得像乞丐。

text: For you then, breakfast is the most important meal of the day.
zh: 这么说，对你来说，早餐是一天中最重要的一餐。

text: I agree. So it's really important, particularly for people who are trying to maintain a healthy
zh: 没错。这一点非常重要，尤其对那些想维持健康体重

text: weight and people who are trying to lose weight. Alexandra says that you should breakfast like a king and dine like a pauper.
zh: 或想减肥的人来说更是如此。亚历山德拉说，早餐应该吃得像国王，晚餐应该吃得像乞丐（pauper）。

text: is an old -fashioned word for a very poor person.
zh: pauper是一个较为古旧的词，指的是非常贫穷的人。

text: The expression means have a big breakfast and a small dinner.
zh: 这个说法的意思是：早餐吃得丰盛些，晚餐吃得简单些。

text: We're going to find out some of the reasons that Alexandra and other experts recommend doing this.
zh: 接下来我们将了解亚历山德拉和其他专家为何推荐这样做的一些原因。

text: And as usual, we'll learn some useful new words and phrases.
zh: 和往常一样，我们也会学到一些实用的新词汇和短语。

text: And remember, you'll find all the vocabulary and a quiz on our website, bbclearningenglish .com.
zh: 别忘了，你可以在我们的网站bbclearningenglish.com上找到所有词汇讲解和小测验。

text: OK, but first I have a question for you, Phil.
zh: 好的，不过在开始之前，我先问你一个问题，菲尔。

text: According to a recent survey, which of the following is the most popular breakfast in the UK?
zh: 根据最近的一项调查，以下哪种是英国最受欢迎的早餐？

text: Is it A. Eggs, B. Toast, or C. Cereal?
zh: 是A鸡蛋，B吐司，还是C麦片？

text: Well, I think people like to keep it simple, so I'm going to say toast.
zh: 嗯，我觉得人们更喜欢简单一点，所以我猜是吐司。

text: OK, well, we'll find out the answer later in the programme.
zh: 好，我们稍后会在节目中揭晓答案。

text: Let's hear some reasons for why we should breakfast like a king or queen. Courtney Peterson, who researches eating behaviours,
zh: 我们先来听听为什么应该像国王一样吃早餐的原因。研究饮食行为的考特尼·彼得森

text: explains how the time of day affects how our bodies process food on BBC World's service programme, The Food Chain.
zh: 在BBC世界服务节目《The Food Chain》中，解释了一天中的时间是如何影响身体对食物的处理方式的。

text: And so if you can eat a large breakfast in the morning, and or I would even say a large lunch in the morning
zh: 如果你能在早上吃一顿丰盛的早餐，甚至可以说是丰盛的午餐，

text: your body's going to be better at metabolizing that food and what that means is your body's gonna actually your blood sugar levels won't spike as high
zh: 你的身体就能更好地代谢这些食物，也就是说，你的血糖水平不会飙升得那么高，

text: you'll you might burn slightly more fat you might burn slightly more calories from eating earlier in the day
zh: 你可能会燃烧稍多一点的脂肪，燃烧稍多一点的热量——如果你在一天中较早的时候进食的话。

text: whereas if you had if you eat most of your food later in the day your metabolism is actually slower later in the day
zh: 而如果你把大部分食物留到一天中较晚的时候吃，你的新陈代谢在那个时候其实是变慢的，

text: so for instance if you ate the same I don't know bowl of ice cream
zh: 举个例子，比如说你吃了同样的一碗冰淇淋，

text: in the morning the afternoon in the evening your blood sugar levels will spike significantly higher in the afternoon and the evening.
zh: 分别在早上、下午和晚上吃，那么在下午和晚上，你的血糖水平会飙升得明显更高。

text: We heard the noun metabolism. This is a word that refers to the chemical processes in your body,
zh: 我们听到了名词metabolism（新陈代谢）。这个词指的是你体内发生的一系列化学过程，

text: and we most often use it to talk about those that convert food into energy.
zh: 我们最常用它来指那些把食物转化为能量的过程。

text: And we also heard the verb form metabolise.
zh: 我们还听到了它的动词形式metabolise（新陈代谢，代谢）。

text: Courtney talked about blood sugar levels spiking. A spike is a sharp, pointed shape.
zh: 考特尼提到了血糖水平的“飙升”（spiking）。spike本意是一种尖锐的、突起的形状。

text: If you see it on a line graph, it means that something has increased very rapidly.
zh: 如果你在折线图上看到它，就意味着某个数值急剧上升了。

text: We often use the noun spike to mean a sudden increase in a value that is being measured.
zh: 我们常用名词spike来表示某个被测量的数值突然增加。

text: The verb spike means to suddenly increase. We've heard that eating earlier in the day can be healthier for us.
zh: 动词spike的意思是“突然上升”。我们已经听说，早点吃东西可能对我们的健康更有益。

text: Now people often don't eat breakfast, sometimes because they don't have time, sometimes because they're trying to save money.
zh: 不过现在很多人经常不吃早饭，有时是因为没时间，有时是为了省钱。

text: Let's hear again from Professor Alexandra Johnston talking to BBC World Service's The Food Jane.
zh: 我们再来听一次亚历山德拉·约翰斯顿教授在BBC世界服务节目《The Food Chain》中的讲话。

text: Now breakfast skipping is interesting because
zh: 跳过早餐这个现象很有意思，因为

text: my own work would tend to suggest and that sort of observational large -scale scale
zh: 我自己的研究倾向于表明——而且大规模的

text: epidemiological studies would also support this that people who
zh: 流行病学观察性研究也支持这一点——那些

text: have a regular breakfast are actually leaner
zh: 经常吃早餐的人体型其实更瘦，

text: and my own work would tend to suggest that having your largest meal of the day in the morning
zh: 而且我自己的研究也倾向于表明，如果把一天中最丰盛的一餐放在早上吃，

text: period gives you a much better appetite control which then means it's much easier
zh: 就能更好地控制食欲，这样一来

text: to control food intake later in the day.
zh: 之后一天里控制食量也会容易得多。

text: Alexandra starts by mentioning breakfast skipping. If you skip something, then you don't do it.
zh: 亚历山德拉一开始提到了“跳过早餐”（breakfast skipping）。如果你skip某件事，就是说你没有去做它。

text: Bad students might skip lessons, they don't go to them. If you skip a meal, it means that you don't eat it.
zh: 不好好学习的学生可能会skip课，也就是不去上课。如果你skip一顿饭，意思就是你没有吃这顿饭。

text: We also heard that people who have a regular breakfast are leaner. If someone is lean, then they're not fat.
zh: 我们还听到，经常吃早餐的人体型更瘦（leaner）。如果一个人是lean的，那他就不胖。

text: And one of the reasons given why people might be leaner is that they have better appetite control.
zh: 其中一个被提到的原因是，他们对食欲有更好的控制。

text: Appetite control is where your brain decides how much you should eat.
zh: 食欲控制（appetite control）指的是你的大脑决定你应该吃多少的过程。

text: Thanks, Georgie. Now, I think it's time that we heard the answer to your question. Yes, it is.
zh: 谢谢你，乔治。现在我想是时候公布你那个问题的答案了。没错，是时候了。

text: OK, I asked, according to a recent survey, what is the most popular breakfast in the UK? And I said toast!
zh: 好的，我之前问：根据最近的一项调查，英国最受欢迎的早餐是什么？我当时说的是吐司！

text: Which was the most popular in London, but the most popular in the country as a whole was cereal,
zh: 在伦敦确实是吐司最受欢迎，但就全国范围来看，最受欢迎的其实是麦片，

text: and that's according to a survey by a cereal manufacturer. So I'm afraid you got it wrong.
zh: 这是根据一家麦片生产商所做的调查得出的结论。所以很遗憾，你答错了。

text: OK, let's recap the vocabulary we've learned, starting with pauper, which is an old -fashioned word for a very poor person.
zh: 好，我们来回顾一下今天学到的词汇，先从pauper开始，这是一个较为古旧的词，指非常贫穷的人。

text: When we're talking about data, it spikes when it increases suddenly.
zh: 当我们谈论数据时，如果它突然大幅上升，就可以说它spikes（飙升）。

text: Metabolism refers to the chemical processes inside your body, especially those which process food.
zh: metabolism指的是你体内的化学过程，尤其是处理食物的那些过程。

text: If you skip something, you miss it, you don't do it.
zh: 如果你skip某件事，就是说你错过了它，没有去做。

text: If someone is lean, then they are not fat. And finally, appetite control is how your brain decides how much you should eat.
zh: 如果一个人是lean的，那他就不胖。最后，appetite control指的是你的大脑决定你应该吃多少的过程。

text: Once again, our six minutes are up, but why not head over to our website, bbclearningenglish .com,
zh: 我们的六分钟又结束了，不妨去我们的网站bbclearningenglish.com，

text: to try the quiz and worksheet for this episode. See you again soon. Goodbye. Bye.
zh: 试试这一期的小测验和练习题吧。我们下次再见，拜拜。拜拜。
